description: NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee recession dates. The NBER peak date marks the start of economic contraction; trough marks the end. Equity markets typically lead the NBER declaration by 1-6 months — the convergence test therefore anchors on the first SPY -15% drawdown within the window, not the NBER peak itself. The 2022 bear market is intentionally excluded: NBER never declared a recession, and a rate-hike-driven grinding bear is trendDrawdown territory, not acute crisis.

eventWindows.label: NBER recession: peak Dec 2007 → trough Jun 2009. SPY peak Oct 9 2007, trough Mar 9 2009 (-56.8%).
NBER recession: peak Feb 2020 → trough Apr 2020. SPY peak Feb 19 2020, trough Mar 23 2020 (-33.9%).

passCriterion: Detector fires within 10 trading days of SPY crossing -15% drawdown from its 252-day high (anchor computed inside the window). Coverage >= 40% of window required — NBER recessions span longer than the acute panic phase, so full coverage is not expected from a panic-oriented detector.
